name: Verify NIXL LIBFABRIC Backend Fix
description: End-to-end verification of the LIBFABRIC plugin build, deployment, and backend initialization. Validates build artifacts, S3 deployment, node installation, backend creation, and API functionality.

tasks.name: Verify Build Artifact Exists
tasks.description: Check that the built LIBFABRIC plugin SO file exists in the NIXL build directory
commands: test -f "${BUILD_PATH}" || { echo "[FAIL] Build artifact not found: ${BUILD_PATH}"; echo '[HINT] Run: cd ~/nixl/build && ninja'; exit 1; }

tasks.name: Verify Build Artifact Size (~548KB)
tasks.description: Ensure the plugin file size is within expected range (500-600KB), indicating a complete build
commands: if [ "${SIZE_KB}" -lt {{EXPECTED_MIN_SIZE_KB}} ]; then echo "[FAIL] Plugin too small (${SIZE_KB} KB < {{EXPECTED_MIN_SIZE_KB}} KB). Build may be incomplete."; exit 1; fi
if [ "${SIZE_KB}" -gt {{EXPECTED_MAX_SIZE_KB}} ]; then echo "[WARN] Plugin larger than expected (${SIZE_KB} KB > {{EXPECTED_MAX_SIZE_KB}} KB). Verify build is correct."; fi
echo "[OK] Plugin size within expected range: ${SIZE_KB} KB"

tasks.name: Verify Build Artifact Symbol Table
tasks.description: Check that the plugin contains the conn_info_ symbol, confirming the two-sided messaging implementation
commands: echo '[INFO] Checking symbol table for conn_info_...'

nm -D "${BUILD_PATH}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'conn_info_' && echo '[OK] conn_info_ symbol found' || { echo '[FAIL] conn_info_ symbol not found in plugin. Two-sided messaging may not be compiled in.'; exit 1; }

for SYM in nixlBackendInit nixlBackendCleanup; do nm -D "${BUILD_PATH}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "${SYM}" && echo "[OK] ${SYM} found" || { echo "[FAIL] ${SYM} not found"; MISSING=$((MISSING + 1)); }; done
if [ "${MISSING}" -gt 0 ]; then echo "[FAIL] ${MISSING} required symbol(s) missing"; exit 1; fi

tasks.name: Verify Build Artifact Timestamp
tasks.description: Confirm the build artifact is recent (modified within the last 24 hours)
commands: if [ "${AGE_HOURS}" -gt 24 ]; then echo "[WARN] Build artifact is ${AGE_HOURS} hours old. Consider rebuilding: cd ~/nixl/build && ninja"; else echo '[OK] Build artifact is recent'; fi

tasks.name: Verify S3 Deployment
tasks.description: Check that the plugin exists in S3 and its size matches the local build
commands: aws s3 ls "${S3_URI}" --region us-west-2 > /tmp/s3_plugin_info.txt 2>&1 || { echo "[FAIL] Plugin not found in S3: ${S3_URI}"; echo '[HINT] Upload with: aws s3 cp ~/nixl/build/src/plugins/libfabric/{{PLUGIN_NAME}} ${S3_URI} --region us-west-2'; exit 1; }
echo "[INFO] S3 size: ${S3_SIZE} bytes, Local size: ${LOCAL_SIZE} bytes"
if [ "${S3_SIZE}" != "${LOCAL_SIZE}" ]; then echo "[FAIL] Size mismatch! S3=${S3_SIZE}, Local=${LOCAL_SIZE}. Re-upload required."; exit 1; fi
echo '[OK] S3 deployment verified: size matches local build'

tasks.name: Verify Node1 Plugin Installation
tasks.description: SSH to Node1 and verify the plugin is installed at the correct path with correct permissions
commands: if [ -z "${NIXL_DIR}" ]; then echo '[FAIL] NIXL not installed on Node1'; exit 1; fi
test -f "${PLUGIN_PATH}" || { echo "[FAIL] Plugin not found: ${PLUGIN_PATH}"; exit 1; }
if [ "${PERMS}" != '755' ]; then echo "[WARN] Permissions are ${PERMS}, expected 755"; fi
nm -D "${PLUGIN_PATH}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'conn_info_' && echo '[OK] Node1: conn_info_ symbol present' || echo '[FAIL] Node1: conn_info_ symbol missing'

tasks.name: Verify Node2 Plugin Installation
tasks.description: SSH to Node2 and verify the plugin is installed at the correct path with correct permissions
commands: if [ -z "${NIXL_DIR}" ]; then echo '[FAIL] NIXL not installed on Node2'; exit 1; fi
test -f "${PLUGIN_PATH}" || { echo "[FAIL] Plugin not found: ${PLUGIN_PATH}"; exit 1; }
if [ "${PERMS}" != '755' ]; then echo "[WARN] Permissions are ${PERMS}, expected 755"; fi
nm -D "${PLUGIN_PATH}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'conn_info_' && echo '[OK] Node2: conn_info_ symbol present' || echo '[FAIL] Node2: conn_info_ symbol missing'

tasks.name: Verify Plugin Size Matches Across Nodes
tasks.description: Ensure both nodes have the same plugin file (by size) and it matches the local build
commands: echo '[INFO] Comparing plugin sizes across build, Node1, and Node2...'

NODE1_SIZE=$(ssh -i {{SSH_KEY}} -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ubuntu@{{NODE1_PUBLIC_IP}} 'NIXL_DIR=$(python3 -c "import nixl,os; print(os.path.dirname(nixl.__file__))"); stat -c %s ${NIXL_DIR}/_lib/{{PLUGIN_NAME}}')

NODE2_SIZE=$(ssh -i {{SSH_KEY}} -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ubuntu@{{NODE2_PUBLIC_IP}} 'NIXL_DIR=$(python3 -c "import nixl,os; print(os.path.dirname(nixl.__file__))"); stat -c %s ${NIXL_DIR}/_lib/{{PLUGIN_NAME}}')
if [ "${LOCAL_SIZE}" != "${NODE1_SIZE}" ]; then echo '[FAIL] Node1 size mismatch with local build'; MISMATCH=1; fi
if [ "${LOCAL_SIZE}" != "${NODE2_SIZE}" ]; then echo '[FAIL] Node2 size mismatch with local build'; MISMATCH=1; fi
if [ "${NODE1_SIZE}" != "${NODE2_SIZE}" ]; then echo '[FAIL] Node1 and Node2 sizes differ'; MISMATCH=1; fi
if [ "${MISMATCH}" -eq 1 ]; then echo '[FAIL] Plugin sizes do not match. Re-deploy required.'; exit 1; fi

tasks.name: Verify Producer (Node1) is Running
tasks.description: Check that the vLLM Producer process is running on Node1 with the LIBFABRIC backend
commands: if [ -z "${VLLM_PID}" ]; then echo '[FAIL] Producer vLLM process not running'; exit 1; fi
ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ':{{PRODUCER_PORT}}' | head -1 || { echo '[FAIL] Producer not listening on port {{PRODUCER_PORT}}'; exit 1; }
echo '[OK] Producer is running on port {{PRODUCER_PORT}}'

tasks.name: Verify Consumer (Node2) is Running
tasks.description: Check that the vLLM Consumer process is running on Node2 with the LIBFABRIC backend
commands: if [ -z "${VLLM_PID}" ]; then echo '[FAIL] Consumer vLLM process not running'; exit 1; fi
ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ':{{CONSUMER_PORT}}' | head -1 || { echo '[FAIL] Consumer not listening on port {{CONSUMER_PORT}}'; exit 1; }
echo '[OK] Consumer is running on port {{CONSUMER_PORT}}'

tasks.name: Verify LIBFABRIC Backend on Producer
tasks.description: Check Producer logs for 'Backend LIBFABRIC was instantiated' and EFA device discovery
commands: echo '[INFO] Checking Producer LIBFABRIC backend logs...'
echo '[INFO] No explicit log file found. Checking process stderr...'
echo '[INFO] Checking for EFA device discovery...'

if grep -q 'Discovered.*EFA devices' "${LOG_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then echo '[OK] EFA devices discovered'; CHECKS_OK=$((CHECKS_OK+1)); else echo '[WARN] EFA device discovery not found in logs'; CHECKS_FAIL=$((CHECKS_FAIL+1)); fi
echo '[INFO] Checking for data rail creation...'

if grep -q 'Created data rail.*provider=efa' "${LOG_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then echo '[OK] Data rail created with EFA'; CHECKS_OK=$((CHECKS_OK+1)); else echo '[WARN] Data rail creation not found in logs'; CHECKS_FAIL=$((CHECKS_FAIL+1)); fi
echo '[INFO] Checking for control rail creation...'

if grep -q 'Created control rail.*provider=efa' "${LOG_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then echo '[OK] Control rail created with EFA'; CHECKS_OK=$((CHECKS_OK+1)); else echo '[WARN] Control rail creation not found in logs'; CHECKS_FAIL=$((CHECKS_FAIL+1)); fi

if grep -q 'NIXL_ERR_INVALID_PARAM' "${LOG_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then echo '[FAIL] NIXL_ERR_INVALID_PARAM found in Producer logs!'; CHECKS_FAIL=$((CHECKS_FAIL+1)); else echo '[OK] No NIXL_ERR_INVALID_PARAM errors'; CHECKS_OK=$((CHECKS_OK+1)); fi
echo "[INFO] Producer backend checks: ${CHECKS_OK} passed, ${CHECKS_FAIL} issues"
if [ "${CHECKS_FAIL}" -gt 1 ]; then echo '[FAIL] Multiple backend issues detected'; exit 1; fi
echo '[OK] Producer LIBFABRIC backend verification complete'

tasks.name: Verify LIBFABRIC Backend on Consumer
tasks.description: Check Consumer logs for backend instantiation and connection establishment
commands: echo '[INFO] Checking Consumer LIBFABRIC backend logs...'
echo '[INFO] No explicit log file found. Checking process stderr...'
echo '[INFO] Checking for EFA device discovery...'

if grep -q 'Discovered.*EFA devices' "${LOG_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then echo '[OK] EFA devices discovered'; CHECKS_OK=$((CHECKS_OK+1)); else echo '[WARN] EFA device discovery not found in logs'; CHECKS_FAIL=$((CHECKS_FAIL+1)); fi
echo '[INFO] Checking for data rail creation...'

if grep -q 'Created data rail.*provider=efa' "${LOG_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then echo '[OK] Data rail created with EFA'; CHECKS_OK=$((CHECKS_OK+1)); else echo '[WARN] Data rail creation not found in logs'; CHECKS_FAIL=$((CHECKS_FAIL+1)); fi

if grep -q 'Successfully created connection' "${LOG_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then echo '[OK] Connection established'; CHECKS_OK=$((CHECKS_OK+1)); else echo '[WARN] Connection establishment not found in logs'; CHECKS_FAIL=$((CHECKS_FAIL+1)); fi

if grep -q 'NIXL_ERR_INVALID_PARAM' "${LOG_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then echo '[FAIL] NIXL_ERR_INVALID_PARAM found in Consumer logs!'; CHECKS_FAIL=$((CHECKS_FAIL+1)); else echo '[OK] No NIXL_ERR_INVALID_PARAM errors'; CHECKS_OK=$((CHECKS_OK+1)); fi

if grep -q 'EAGAIN' "${LOG_FILE}" 2>/dev/null; then echo '[FAIL] fi_read EAGAIN found in Consumer logs! Two-sided messaging fix may not be applied.'; CHECKS_FAIL=$((CHECKS_FAIL+1)); else echo '[OK] No EAGAIN errors'; CHECKS_OK=$((CHECKS_OK+1)); fi
echo "[INFO] Consumer backend checks: ${CHECKS_OK} passed, ${CHECKS_FAIL} issues"
if [ "${CHECKS_FAIL}" -gt 1 ]; then echo '[FAIL] Multiple backend issues detected'; exit 1; fi
echo '[OK] Consumer LIBFABRIC backend verification complete'

tasks.name: Verify Producer API Health
tasks.description: Send a health check request to the Producer API endpoint
commands: echo '[INFO] Testing Producer API health on Node1 ({{NODE1_PUBLIC_IP}}:{{PRODUCER_PORT}})...'
if [ "${RESPONSE}" = '200' ]; then echo '[OK] Producer API is healthy (HTTP 200)'; else echo "[FAIL] Producer API returned HTTP ${RESPONSE}"; exit 1; fi

tasks.name: Verify Consumer API Health
tasks.description: Send a health check request to the Consumer API endpoint
commands: echo '[INFO] Testing Consumer API health on Node2 ({{NODE2_PUBLIC_IP}}:{{CONSUMER_PORT}})...'
if [ "${RESPONSE}" = '200' ]; then echo '[OK] Consumer API is healthy (HTTP 200)'; else echo "[FAIL] Consumer API returned HTTP ${RESPONSE}"; exit 1; fi

tasks.name: Verify Producer Simple Inference
tasks.description: Send a simple inference request to Producer and verify response
commands: echo '[INFO] Sending test inference request to Producer...'
if [ -z "${RESULT}" ]; then echo '[FAIL] No response from Producer'; exit 1; fi

echo "${RESULT}" | python3 -c 'import json,sys; r=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f"[INFO] Response: id={r.get("id","N/A")}, model={r.get("model","N/A")}"); assert "choices" in r, "No choices in response"; assert len(r["choices"]) > 0, "Empty choices"; print(f"[INFO] Generated text: {r["choices"][0].get("text","")[:50]}"); print("[OK] Producer inference successful")' || { echo '[FAIL] Producer inference returned invalid response'; echo "[DEBUG] Response: ${RESULT}"; exit 1; }

tasks.name: Verify Consumer Simple Inference
tasks.description: Send a simple inference request to Consumer and verify response
commands: echo '[INFO] Sending test inference request to Consumer...'
if [ -z "${RESULT}" ]; then echo '[FAIL] No response from Consumer'; exit 1; fi

echo "${RESULT}" | python3 -c 'import json,sys; r=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f"[INFO] Response: id={r.get("id","N/A")}, model={r.get("model","N/A")}"); assert "choices" in r, "No choices in response"; assert len(r["choices"]) > 0, "Empty choices"; print(f"[INFO] Generated text: {r["choices"][0].get("text","")[:50]}"); print("[OK] Consumer inference successful")' || { echo '[FAIL] Consumer inference returned invalid response'; echo "[DEBUG] Response: ${RESULT}"; exit 1; }

tasks.name: Verification Summary
tasks.description: Print a summary of all verification results
commands: echo '    - Both nodes have the plugin'
echo '    - Sizes match across all locations'
echo '    - EFA devices discovered on both nodes'
echo '    - Data/Control rails created with provider=efa'
echo '    - Producer API healthy and serving inference'
echo '    - Consumer API healthy and serving inference'
echo 'Next step: Run disaggregated inference test via Proxy Server'
